title: Integrate telemetry into scheduler _finalize and bead metadata
description: In scheduler.py _finalize(), after processing agent result and before marking bead status: (1) extract metrics from agent_result.telemetry into bead.metadata['telemetry'] (latest attempt, overwritten each run), (2) append metrics to bead.metadata['telemetry_history'] array capped at N entries (default 10, configurable via ORCHESTRATOR_TELEMETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS env var — invalid/zero/negative values fall back to default), (3) derive attempt number from len(telemetry_history) + 1, (4) call storage.write_telemetry_artifact() with full artifact data including prompt_text, response_text, parsed_result, timestamps, and error, (5) wrap telemetry writes in try/except — if write fails, add warning to execution_history but preserve bead outcome. Telemetry must be captured for all outcomes: completed, blocked, and failed. Handle the case where agent_result.telemetry is None gracefully (skip telemetry storage).
acceptance_criteria: bead.metadata['telemetry'] contains latest attempt metrics after each run
bead.metadata['telemetry_history'] contains array of all attempt metrics
telemetry_history capped at 10 entries by default (oldest pruned after append)
Cap configurable via ORCHESTRATOR_TELEMETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS env var
Invalid/zero/negative env var values fall back to default 10
Attempt number derived from len(telemetry_history) + 1
Full artifact file written via storage.write_telemetry_artifact()
Telemetry captured for completed, blocked, and failed outcomes
Telemetry write failure adds warning to execution_history but does not affect bead outcome
Gracefully handles agent_result.telemetry being None

handoff_summary.completed: Full test suite (303 tests) passed across all background runs.

execution_history.summary: All done. Full test suite (303 tests) passed across all background runs. The bead is complete.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run only the specific test files directly related to your changes, not the full test suite. Use `uv run python -m unittest tests.<module_name> -v` to target individual test files rather than `discover`. Leave comprehensive test validation to the tester agent.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
